public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as assistant curator.
manner: Keeps language exact, as if wording itself is part of the custody chain. In Shirsh, that reads as someone investigative, magically literate, and discreet, balancing inquiry with regulation, and the habits of a assistant curator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of assistant curator in and around Shirshal, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Shirshal workable through the obligations of assistant curator while managing the immediate pressure of magical law violations in Shirsh.
hidden_pressure: Must preserve sequence and custody even when powerful parties would prefer ambiguity. Around Shirshal, that usually means carrying magical law violations, magical compliance, institutional scrutiny without letting the role of assistant curator visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Nira Veilthorn can entangle the party in seals, claims, or proof requirements that others are trying to blur on purpose.
Nira Veilthorn can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Nira Veilthorn communicates in public. In Shirshal, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Magi Magistratus.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Shirshal: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague
